multiplexing_model.source_claims: Multiple-excitation scheme 'pushes the branching ratio' of the 866 nm transition from the single-mode 6% toward unity; the optimal effective branching ratio achievable is BR_max = 54.4% (limited by ion decay to |D_3/2> during optical pumping), saturating at N~=10 time-bin modes. The realized method also reports 'roughly a 5-fold enhancement in efficiency compared to the single-mode case'.
multiplexing_model.model_use: SeQUeNCe repeater backend only. SeQUeNCe Memory has a single per-attempt efficiency scalar and no native multiplexing, so the multiplexed node is approximated by substituting the effective branching ratio for the single-mode 6% via the repeater builder's memory_efficiency_override. Selected value: effective_branching_ratio (BR_max = 0.544, ideal saturated). The active parameters.efficiency (0.06) is left unchanged so the compiled/direct backends keep the single-mode value.
multiplexing_model.mapping_assumption: The paper's effective 866 nm branching ratio is mapped 1:1 onto SeQUeNCe Memory.efficiency (per-attempt photon-emission/branching probability). Meet-in-the-middle BSM then squares it across the two link arms, as it did for the 6% single-mode value.

memory_process_model.calibration_status: Cui2025 reports heralded ion-photon Bell-state fidelities and memory-storage benchmarks, but does not supply a calibrated constructor/interface process matrix or per-memory-qubit Pauli/depolarizing channel; no constructor/interface process noise is applied by default

ion_photon_entanglement_benchmark.quantity: heralded ion-photon Bell-state fidelity to the closest maximally entangled state
ion_photon_entanglement_benchmark.model_use: benchmark/provenance only; not consumed as memory_process_model and not mapped to per-memory-qubit depolarizing noise

memory_storage_decoherence_model.coherence_time_definition: source-reported Gaussian fit parameter; exact 1/e/sigma convention not independently rederived here

units.fidelity: dimensionless in [0, 1] (reported memory-state/storage benchmark; not a process probability)
units.efficiency: dimensionless in [0, 1] (per-excitation 866-nm photon branch / single-excitation probability)
units.multiplexing_model: typed multiplexing descriptor; effective_branching_ratio is dimensionless in [0, 1] and is the SeQUeNCe-repeater-only substitute for the single-mode efficiency (does not modify parameters.efficiency)
units.coherence_time: s (reported memory coherence time; source fit is Gaussian)
units.memory_process_model: typed memory/interface process model descriptor; kind=none for the Cui profile because the paper does not report a calibrated process channel
units.ion_photon_entanglement_benchmark: dimensionless fidelity benchmark/provenance object; not an active process parameter
units.detected_decay_lifetime_s: s (fitted |D_5/2> lifetime; spontaneous decay is detected by mid-circuit measurement and converts to heralded loss booked as no_delivery failure stage memory_decay_detected; consumed by the kernel as per-memory survival exp(-storage age / lifetime))
units.endpoint_analysis_rotation_model: typed endpoint analysis-rotation error descriptor (non-Z settings only); TODO(scientific): kind=none because no node-specific single-qubit analysis-pulse fidelity is reported in the archived sources
units.memory_storage_decoherence_model: typed stationary-memory storage decoherence model descriptor
units.memory_readout_model: typed endpoint memory-readout assignment model descriptor

photon_wavepacket.source: Cui2025 arXiv:2503.13898 (P_1/2 spontaneous decay time 7 ns; free-space emission)
photon_wavepacket.indistinguishability_note: the source paper argues matched exponential wavepackets from two free-space ions give indistinguishable photons for the two-photon BSM (App. C); residual mismatch is not quantified, so photon_indistinguishability_model stays kind=none (mode_overlap 1)
photon_wavepacket.model_use: metadata for HOM overlap decisions; not consumed by the kernel

notes: Parameter names mirror sequence.components.memory.Memory where possible. Literature source: Cui et al., A metropolitan-scale trapped-ion quantum network node with hybrid multiplexing enhancements, arXiv:2503.13898. This profile models the reported Ca-40 trapped-ion network-node memory/communication interface: 93.3% memory-qubit fidelity after 100 ms storage is retained as a storage/benchmark datum and is not a process-channel probability; consecutive excitation pulses at 13 ns give a 76 MHz excitation cadence; the 866-nm photon branch has 6% single-excitation probability; and the fitted memory-qubit coherence time 366 ms is converted to 0.366 s. The paper also discusses an effective 54.4% branching ratio under repeated excitation; that multiplexing improvement is not used for SeQUeNCe Memory.efficiency because this field is per excitation. Decoherence-channel options (decoherence_errors, cutoff_ratio) remain omitted because this source reports a Gaussian decay fit, not a Pauli error distribution. The reported ion-photon entanglement fidelities of 96.8% / 94.6% / 89.8% at 3 m / 1 km / 12 km are stored as ion_photon_entanglement_benchmark provenance only. They are tomography-reconstructed Bell-state fidelities for the heralded ion-photon interface, not calibrated per-memory-qubit depolarizing probabilities, not final repeater-chain fidelities, and not applied as active constructor/interface process noise. The explicit memory_process_model is therefore kind=none by default. The memory_storage_decoherence_model is Gaussian dephasing (coherence measured by Ramsey-with-echo, fitted F = a*exp(-(t/tau)^2)+1/2 with tau = 366 ms), a one-parameter storage approximation. The 958 ms |D_5/2> lifetime is recorded as detected_decay_lifetime_s: the paper detects spontaneous-decay errors with a mid-circuit measurement (survival 89%/79%/74% at 100/240/300 ms), so this channel is heralded loss that belongs in delivery/no-delivery mass, not in the storage decoherence state channel; the kernel does not consume it yet. The explicit memory_readout_model is none: ion readout and analysis-rotation fidelities are not separately quantified in the archived source (the 98.5%/98.2%/96.7% merging fidelities are subspace-conversion operations, not endpoint readout), so endpoint readout stays ideal rather than miscalibrated.
